summary: End-to-end testing of Maintenance Scheduler & Planning Engine (Phase 1d). Backend: 13/14 pytest passed (1 skipped because no scheduled tasks exist for full PATCH/defer lifecycle — acceptable). Frontend: all UI flows (sub-tabs, KPIs, action buttons, dialogs, AI planner LLM call) work without console/page errors.

issues: Duplicate tabs label rendering: the outer wrapper Tabs ('Maintenance Strategy' / 'Maintenance Schedule') is rendered RIGHT ABOVE the inner Tabs in MaintenanceStrategyManager which carries the SAME two labels ('Maintenance Strategy' / 'Maintenance Schedule'). Two identical tab strips stacked vertically look confusing. Suggest hiding the inner duplicate or renaming inner ones (e.g. inner could be 'Strategy Details' or be removed because outer wrapper already controls the switching).

action_items: Optional: deduplicate the nested tab strips inside MaintenanceStrategyManager — the outer Tabs in MaintenanceStrategyTab.jsx already provides Strategy/Schedule switching, so the inner Tabs with the same labels are redundant and visually noisy.
critical_code_review_comments: maintenance_scheduler.py (1005 lines) is approaching the >700 line guideline — consider splitting into modules: programs.py, scheduler.py, tasks.py, ai_planner.py, technicians.py.
POST /ai-plan/apply accepts raw List[dict] which bypasses Pydantic validation. Wrap in a Pydantic model with a `recommendations: List[Recommendation]` field for input safety.
complete_task() returns `next_due_date` only if program found; if not, returns None silently. Consider 404 when program is missing for data integrity signalling.

AI planner system prompt asks LLM to return JSON but there is no schema enforcement — already gracefully handled by try/except, but adding `response_format={'type':'json_object'}` to the OpenAI call would improve reliability.
Frequency-to-days mapping in helper uses 'on_condition' -> 30 which silently masks an explicit configuration choice — log a debug message at minimum.

success_rate.backend: 100% (13/14 passed, 1 skipped due to no seed tasks)

seed_data_creation: Created 1 technician 'TEST_Tech_AI_Planner' via POST /api/maintenance-scheduler/technicians during test. Safe to delete.
context_for_next_testing_agent: AI planner (POST /api/maintenance-scheduler/ai-plan) is real OpenAI gpt-4o via Emergent LLM key — works correctly. When no open scheduled tasks exist in the window the endpoint returns {'message': 'No open tasks to plan', 'recommendations': []} (200) without calling the LLM — this is by design. For deeper lifecycle testing (PATCH /tasks/{id}, /complete) you need scheduled tasks to exist; run POST /run-scheduler after applying a strategy to a piece of equipment. The Library > Maintenance > Equipment Type selection requires clicking an item INSIDE the right-side inner equipment-type list (e.g. 'Brabender'); do NOT click items in the outer left hierarchy sidebar (those are area-level and switch you off the Library page).
rca of the issue: N/A — all features under test are functional. Only finding is a minor visual duplication of tab labels inside MaintenanceStrategyManager when the outer wrapper Tabs in MaintenanceStrategyTab.jsx now provides the same Strategy/Schedule switch.
